root_text.an4.16:1.3: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu rūpasokhummena samannāgato hoti paramena;
root_text.an4.16:1.4: tena ca rūpasokhummena aññaṁ rūpasokhummaṁ uttaritaraṁ vā paṇītataraṁ vā na samanupassati;
root_text.an4.16:1.5: tena ca rūpasokhummena aññaṁ rūpasokhummaṁ uttaritaraṁ vā paṇītataraṁ vā na pattheti.
root_text.an4.16:1.7: tena ca vedanāsokhummena aññaṁ vedanāsokhummaṁ uttaritaraṁ vā paṇītataraṁ vā na samanupassati;
root_text.an4.16:1.8: tena ca vedanāsokhummena aññaṁ vedanāsokhummaṁ uttaritaraṁ vā paṇītataraṁ vā na pattheti.
root_text.an4.16:1.10: tena ca saññāsokhummena aññaṁ saññāsokhummaṁ uttaritaraṁ vā paṇītataraṁ vā na samanupassati;
root_text.an4.16:1.11: tena ca saññāsokhummena aññaṁ saññāsokhummaṁ uttaritaraṁ vā paṇītataraṁ vā na pattheti.
root_text.an4.16:1.13: tena ca saṅkhārasokhummena aññaṁ saṅkhārasokhummaṁ uttaritaraṁ vā paṇītataraṁ vā na samanupassati;
root_text.an4.16:1.14: tena ca saṅkhārasokhummena aññaṁ saṅkhārasokhummaṁ uttaritaraṁ vā paṇītataraṁ vā na pattheti.

translation_text.an4.16:1.1: “Mendicants, there are these four kinds of subtlety.
translation_text.an4.16:1.3: A mendicant has ultimate subtlety of form.
translation_text.an4.16:1.4: They don’t see any other subtlety of form that’s better or finer than that,
translation_text.an4.16:1.5: nor do they aim for it.
translation_text.an4.16:1.6: A mendicant has ultimate subtlety of feeling.
translation_text.an4.16:1.7: They don’t see any other subtlety of feeling that’s better or finer than that,
translation_text.an4.16:1.8: nor do they aim for it.
translation_text.an4.16:1.9: A mendicant has ultimate subtlety of perception.
translation_text.an4.16:1.10: They don’t see any other subtlety of perception that’s better or finer than that,
translation_text.an4.16:1.11: nor do they aim for it.
translation_text.an4.16:1.12: A mendicant has ultimate subtlety of choices.
translation_text.an4.16:1.13: They don’t see any other subtlety of choices that’s better or finer than that,
translation_text.an4.16:1.14: nor do they aim for it.
translation_text.an4.16:1.15: These are the four kinds of subtlety.
translation_text.an4.16:3.2: peaceful, in love with the state of peace.
translation_text.an4.16:3.4: having vanquished Māra with his legions.”

reference_text.an4.16:1.1: bj19.36, cck21.23, csp1ed15.325, csp2ed15.325, ms15A4_108, msdiv16, pts-vp-pli2.18, sya21.23
